topic: spring frost damage to shoots and yield

steps.label: cause
steps.text: Air temperature falls below freezing after tender buds and shoots have emerged.

steps.label: mechanism
steps.text: Ice formation damages living cells in young shoots, leaves, and inflorescences.

steps.label: effect
steps.text: Primary growth and potential flower clusters are lost, which can reduce the season's yield substantially.

steps.label: exam_formulation
steps.text: Formative causal formulation: Primary growth and potential flower clusters are lost, which can reduce the season's yield substantially.

_meta.source_note: Inferred formative model - not official WSET assessment or mark-scheme content
